name: Aphids
symptoms: Small green/black insects clustering on leaves and stems; sticky honeydew secretion; leaf curling and yellowing.
treatment: Spray Imidacloprid 17.8% SL @ 0.25ml/L or Dimethoate 30% EC @ 2ml/L. Use neem oil (5ml/L) for organic control.
prevention: Regular monitoring, avoid excess nitrogen, use resistant varieties, encourage natural predators (ladybirds).

name: Rice Blast
symptoms: Diamond-shaped grey lesions with brown borders on leaves; neck rot causing white/empty panicles; severe yield loss.
treatment: Apply Tricyclazole 75% WP @ 0.6g/L or Isoprothiolane 40% EC @ 1.5ml/L at disease appearance.
prevention: Use blast-resistant varieties (IR-64, Swarnadhan), balanced fertilization, avoid excess nitrogen.

name: Wheat Rust (Yellow/Brown)
symptoms: Yellowish-orange or brown powdery pustules on leaves and stems; leaves turn yellow and dry prematurely.
treatment: Spray Propiconazole 25% EC @ 1ml/L or Tebuconazole 250 EW @ 1ml/L at first sign of disease.
prevention: Plant resistant varieties, early sowing, timely fungicide application.

name: Stem Borer
symptoms: Dead hearts in young plants; white ears (whitehead) in mature plants; circular holes in stem with frass.
treatment: Apply Cartap Hydrochloride 4G @ 18kg/ha or Chlorpyrifos 20% EC @ 2.5ml/L. Use pheromone traps.
prevention: Remove stubble after harvest, balanced fertilization, monitor with pheromone traps.

name: American Bollworm
symptoms: Round holes in cotton bolls; caterpillars feeding inside; damaged and fallen bolls; frass visible.
treatment: Spray Emamectin benzoate 5% SG @ 0.5g/L or Spinosad 45% SC @ 0.3ml/L. Bt spray also effective.
prevention: Use Bt cotton, pheromone traps, inter-cropping, and early planting.

name: Fusarium Wilt
symptoms: Sudden wilting of plants; vascular browning (discoloured vessels seen on stem cut); yellowing of lower leaves.
treatment: Seed treatment with Trichoderma viride @ 4g/kg seed. Soil application of Carbendazim 1g/L.
prevention: Use resistant varieties, crop rotation, avoid waterlogging, soil solarization.

name: Red Spider Mite
symptoms: Tiny red/brown mites on leaf undersides; silver/bronze speckling on leaves; fine webbing; severe infestation causes leaf drop.
treatment: Spray Dicofol 18.5% EC @ 2.5ml/L or Spiromesifen 22.9% SC @ 1ml/L. Avoid synthetic pyrethroids.
prevention: Maintain humidity, avoid over-fertilization with nitrogen, use predatory mites.

name: Leaf Eating Caterpillar
symptoms: Irregular holes and skeletonized leaves; presence of larvae; frass on leaves; defoliation in severe cases.
treatment: Spray Chlorpyrifos 20% EC @ 2ml/L or Quinalphos 25% EC @ 2ml/L. Bt spray for organic control.
prevention: Deep ploughing to expose pupae, use light traps, introduce parasitoids.

name: Leaf Curl Virus
symptoms: Upward/downward curling of leaves; thickened veins; stunted growth; reduced flowering and fruiting.
treatment: No direct cure. Control whitefly vectors: spray Imidacloprid 17.8% SL @ 0.25ml/L. Remove infected plants.
prevention: Use virus-free seeds, control vector (whitefly), plant resistant/tolerant varieties.

name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery coating on leaf surface; yellowing underneath; affected leaves dry and fall; reduced photosynthesis.
treatment: Spray Sulphur 80% WG @ 3g/L or Triadimefon 25% WP @ 1g/L. Karathane WC is also effective.
prevention: Adequate spacing for air circulation, avoid overhead irrigation, use resistant varieties.

name: Whitefly
symptoms: Tiny white winged insects on leaf undersides; yellowing, sticky honeydew; sooty mould growth; transmits viral diseases.
treatment: Spray Thiamethoxam 25% WG @ 0.3g/L or Acetamiprid 20% SP @ 0.2g/L. Yellow sticky traps.
prevention: Use reflective mulches, yellow sticky traps, avoid broad-spectrum insecticides that kill natural enemies.

name: Loose Smut
symptoms: Black powdery masses replacing grain; smut spores spread by wind at ear emergence; entire ear replaced by spores.
treatment: Seed treatment with Carboxin 37.5% + Thiram 37.5% DS @ 2g/kg seed or Tebuconazole 2% DS.
prevention: Use certified disease-free seed, hot water seed treatment (52°C for 10 min).

name: Root Knot Nematode
symptoms: Knot/gall formations on roots; stunted yellowing plants; wilting in afternoon; nutrient deficiency symptoms.
treatment: Apply Carbofuran 3G @ 1kg a.i./ha or Phorate 10G @ 1kg a.i./ha. Neem cake soil application.
prevention: Crop rotation with non-host crops, marigold intercropping, soil solarization, use resistant varieties.

name: Thrips
symptoms: Silvery streaks on leaves; curling, distortion; scarring on fruits; presence of tiny yellowish/dark insects.
treatment: Spray Spinosad 45% SC @ 0.3ml/L or Fipronil 5% SC @ 2ml/L. Blue sticky traps for monitoring.
prevention: Avoid water stress, balanced nutrition, blue sticky traps, reflective mulches.

name: Desert Locust
symptoms: Swarms of large insects; rapid defoliation; complete crop destruction in severe outbreaks.
treatment: Spray Chlorpyrifos 20% EC or Malathion 96% ULV as aerial/ground spray. Report to district agriculture officer immediately.
prevention: Early warning systems, coordinated response, contact local Krishi Vigyan Kendra immediately.
